summary: Sprint 1 (Corporate Library Versioning) full validation — Backend: 20/20 new versioning tests PASS + 52/53 regression pass. Frontend: version badges, VersionHistoryModal, restore buttons all working. Full flow verified: create→v1, update→v2/v3, list versions (desc), restore v1→v4, unicode preservation, RBAC (tecnico blocked / PCM allowed).

minor.issue: Legacy items created BEFORE Sprint 1 rollout have no archived versions — modal shows 'Nenhuma versao encontrada' but item is displayed with badge v1. No auto-backfill for existing rows. Cosmetic — no data corruption. Suggest a one-time migration to seed v1 archive for pre-existing items.

Aggressive rate-limiter (429) affects rapid successive logins in tests. Regression test test_rc41.py::TestAuth::test_login_master_requires_org failed with 429 (was expected 400). Pre-existing infra flake, not related to Sprint 1.

issues: Tab labeled 'Seguranca' (no cedilha ç). Sidebar and page labels use plain ASCII (e.g. 'Historico de Versoes', 'Restaurar', 'Configuracao'). Cosmetic only — user-facing Portuguese should use proper accents.

action_items: (Optional) Add one-time migration/backfill to create v1 archive entries in biblioteca_versoes for procedimentos & seguranca that existed before Sprint 1 (so version modal is never empty).
(Optional) Fix rate-limit-affected regression test test_rc41.py::TestAuth::test_login_master_requires_org — add sleep or use token cache to avoid 429.
(Cosmetic) Restore Portuguese diacritics in tab labels ('Segurança', 'Histórico', 'Configuração', 'Procedimento padrão').
critical_code_review_comments: doc_config.py archive_version() correctly captures snapshot AFTER mutation for create/update/restore — one archive entry per version, matches spec.
Restore endpoint correctly excludes id/organization_id/versao/created_at from snapshot when writing back (avoids clobbering primary keys). Good.
require_editor() reads role and returns (org_id, user_id) — clean helper; RBAC verified for master/admin/pcm allowed and tecnico blocked (403).
GET /versoes has no pagination limit beyond .to_list(100) — currently OK; if any item exceeds 100 versions the oldest may be truncated silently. Consider bumping or making explicit.
biblioteca_versoes documents contain full snapshot dict — could grow unbounded per org over time. Not a blocker for MVP but worth noting for retention policy.
Version modal empty-state handles missing archives correctly ('Nenhuma versao encontrada'), but doesn't hint that current item still has versao=N. Minor UX polish opportunity.
test_credentials: Present at /app/memory/test_credentials.md — validated. NOTE: For master login the org 'ASTEC Cedro' is labeled 'Indústria Demo' in the frontend org list (name mismatch with credentials doc). test.admin@maintrix.com auto-resolves to org 9a232bf2-fc01-4253-813f-8df356be31c1 correctly.
seed_data_creation: Tests created TEST_-prefixed procedimentos and seguranca items during runs. Not cleaned up (following existing test_rc41.py pattern). Manual cleanup via DELETE endpoints if required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint 1 versioning fully working. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_sprint1_versioning.py has 20 tests: TestLogin, TestProcedimentoVersioning (7 sequential tests with class-scoped _proc_id), TestSegurancaVersioning (4), TestRBAC (6), TestUnicodeVersioning (1). Frontend VersionHistoryModal at /config/documentos → Procedimentos/Seguranca tabs → click 'Versoes' button. data-testid pattern: version-history-modal, version-{n}, restore-v{n}, proc-history-{id}, seg-history-{id}. Master login requires org selection in UI (Organização input, ASTEC Cedro shown as 'Indústria Demo' in list — use test.admin@maintrix.com to auto-resolve). Rate limiter is aggressive: space out login calls with 30s sleeps if you see 429.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found in Sprint 1 versioning. Only observation is pre-existing library items (created before Sprint 1) have empty version history (no archive rows exist for them yet). This is expected and self-heals on first update. Root cause: archive_version() only runs on POST/PUT/DELETE/restaurar — not backfilled for existing rows.
